Off Calendar Jun-22-2020 Continued To Jul-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by telephone for Administrators.

Ms. Harrington stated that the accounting is complete and that she will file the inventory, that the matter was remanded to the Court of Appeal regarding the Attorney's fees and that the remittitur was filed on October 31, 2019. The Court questions whether the petition for attorney fees needs to be added back to the calendar. Ms. Harrington stated that she believes the fee issue came through by way of an ex parte petition for authority to retain Counsel which was denied. Status on close of estate is continued to July 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Probate Examiner will communicate with Counsel as to how to get the fee issue before the Court.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Participate In Proceedings To Determine Heirship, Retain Counsel, Enter Into Fee Agreement, And Pay Attorney (San Francisco Local Rules Of Court Section 14.14 J, Prob C 10811, 11704(B))
Off Calendar Jul-30-2018 Advanced From Oct-09-18 To Aug-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Participate In Proceedings To Determine Heirship, Retain Counsel, Enter Into Fee Agreement, And Pay Attorney (San Francisco Local Rules Of Court Section 14.14 J, Prob C 10811, 11704(B))
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Co-Administrators.

The Court stated that the Co-Administrators can go ahead and participate in opposing the appeal if they want to, and questions whether it is appropriate for the Estate to pay for it and whether or not it is for the Co-Administrator's personal benefit as they are Beneficiaries. Ms. Harrington stated that the Heirs of the Decedent's Husband were not entitled to notice by law, that the Probate Examiner required that notice be given to them, that since then they have filed objections to the petition to determine persons entitled to receive when there was only personal property on hand at the time of death, that said petition was before the Court for some time and that they now have filed an appeal. In addition, Ms. Harrington stated that if the Husband's Heirs prevail, it would be extremely difficult to trace what property on hand at the time of death of the first Spouse was personal property and real property, that they would have to do some extensive tracing, that there are about 40 heirs, etc. and that there would be a conflict as to who pays for it. Furthermore, Ms. Harrington stated that an ex parte order to retain Counsel has already been signed, that it is a neutral question and that it's a question of law. The Petition for Authority to Participate in Proceedings to Determine Heirship is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

For Reconsideration Of Order Determining Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Zwieg (via court call) & Paul Melbostad for (objectors)Aleja Ochoa, Encarnacion Ochoa-Torres, Ruben Ochoa-Torres & Rogelio Ochoa-Torres. Amy Harrington for Blanca Valencia & Arnoldo Valencia.

Mr. Zweig presented oral argument citing P.C. 6402.5 regarding a claim by the predeceased spouses heirs at law, that his clients are the siblings and nieces & nephews who are entitled to 1/2 of the Ochoa estate, that the reasons for reconsideration is that the pre-deceased spouse (died in 2008) owned marital residence with his wife on Lake Street, that it was purchased by both in 1986, that they lived there together 22 years until his death, that it was community property, that after his death the decedent continued to live there until she became conserved and moved to a facility, that the Public Guardian determined that other parties had tried to swindle her and obtained a court order putting the property back into her name, the property was sold and funds were deposited and used until her death, asked whether the sale of the property changes the interpretation of 6402.5, stated that the problem here is that the statute does not delineate when personal and real property is to be determined, that the submission is that the property was sold within the 15 years of the pre-deceased spouses death, that there is not one case that interpreted when real property was converted to personal property and that the money deposited into the decedent's account was from the sale of the house. Ms. Harrington stated that this is not a proper motion, that there are no new facts or law, that the original petition was filed over a year before it was heard, that it is inappropriate to appeal the order under this motion, that regarding the merits the Probate Code is clear, that you are to look at what is on hand on the date of death, that the husband died over 6 years prior to the decedent, that there was only personal property on hand at the time of her death, that in this case the data of where the money came from was clear, that in most cases it would not be and would be a difficult tracking scheme, that regarding the equity issues there is no allowance or case on point regarding the code written, that at the time of the hearing the Court was asked to include findings in its order and the objectors were entitled to appeal. Mr. Melbostad stated that the Court has the inherent authority to correct its ruling. Mr. Zwieg stated that regarding the indication that 1008 was not complied with, it asks for different facts not new facts, that they did present different law than what was applied at the hearing, that regarding 6205 the burden to determine the source of personal property is placed on the proponent, that it is clear that no extra work needs to be done, that it was undisputed that this was community property and that only at the end did it convert to personal property. The Court requested that respective proposed orders be submitted no later than September 12, 2016 and that the Motion for Reconsideration will be under submission as of that date. Ms. Harrington requested that if the Court finds that the objectors are entitled to shares in the estate it include the findings in the order.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Saephan. Court Reporter: Nancy E. Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 800-288-3376 (Reported).

For Reconsideration Of Order Determining Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jul-28-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Proceeding To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Blanca Valencia & Arnoldo Valencia. Paul Melbostad for the heirs of the predeceased spouse.

Mr. Melbostad stated that it is undisputed that 50% of the proceeds of the sale go to the heirs of the predeceased spouse. Ms. Harrington stated that the spouse died prior to Ms. Ochoa's death, that the real property was not on hand at the time of death and that Mr. Melbostad does not provide citation to any cases. Mr. Melbostad stated that there are a number of cases that discuss this and referred to the Adams case and that Obdalla case. Ms. Harrington stated that she and Mr. Melbostad have discussed that this is a question of law, cited 6402.5 and that they would like an order that states the findings. The Court took the proceeding to determine distribution rights under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for (petitioner) Guadalupe Ochoa.

The Court asked why written responses to examiner's notes have not been filed. Ms. Harrington stated that the only thing on hand is personal property, that they are trying to locate heirs on the husband's side to send notice and that they need a continuance as many of the heirs are in Mexico. The Court requested that response to the examiner's notes be filed within two weeks. Ms. Harrington stated that the only assets that were in the conservatorship were cash, that they have filed a partial inventory, that they are waiting for the proof required on the genealogy and asked if a declaration from the husband's brother would be sufficient. The proceeding to determine distribution rights was continued to August 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
